markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Create fixtures for text, code, JSON, malformed JSON, PDF missing text, media transcript, moved file, and denied secret file. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: SourceIngestionFixtures leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: SourceIngestionFixtures is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 cc13ab3c; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: E2E IS NOT TAUTOLOGICAL (the prompt's core MT-096 concern): mt096_mixed_root_full_pass asserts BOTH per-file outcomes (each of 10 files: status, error_class, spans individually via outcome(&summary, path)) AND the rolled-up finish-event counts AND the repair-queue population BY EXACT source_id AND a raw-secret leak probe. The ledger count asserts CANNOT pass with broken per-file logic because (a) summary.outcomes.len()==10 with walk_errors.is_empty(), (b) each file's status is asserted before the count rollup, (c) the repair assert lists exact source_ids (broken.json/scanned.pdf/vtt queued; secret+blob NOT queued), not just a number. Strong.

FIXTURE HYGIENE / QUIET / PORTABILITY: committed corpus is small TEXT files only (src/main.rs, overview.md, contract.json, broken.json, clip.srt, clip_partial.vtt, ops/leaked_key.md, blobs/blob.bin). NO committed binary PDFs — both PDFs are GENERATED at test time via knowledge_ingestion::pdf::fixtures (real lopdf PDFs). NO absolute machine paths committed: fixture root via CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR, runtime root via tempfile::tempdir(). leaked_key.md uses the DOCUMENTED FAKE AWS example key + dummy PEM body, explicitly labelled FAKE — no real credential committed. Tests are headless (no foreground window). All clean.

COVERAGE GAP (inherited from MT-091): the e2e secret fixture ops/leaked_key.md exercises ONLY the HIGH/block path (raw-key probe over spans). NO fixture exercises a MEDIUM secret split across a 120-line code-window boundary, nor a pattern-missed secret (github_pat_/xapp_/bare base64 key/JWT split). The 'raw secret nowhere' probe in this e2e therefore does NOT cover the MT-091 cross-span leak. RECOMMEND adding a fixture: a code file >120 lines with a MEDIUM secret straddling the window boundary, asserting the secret is NOT present in any span content (this would currently FAIL, exposing the MT-091 hole).
COUNT LOCK: asserts success==5/partial==1/failed==2/blocked==1/skipped==1 — locks the corpus shape; any added fixture must update these. Acceptable for a fixed corpus.
evasion_probes: every file gets a typed receipt; no walk_errors/invalid_paths CONFIRMED.
image-only pdf -> NO_TEXT_LAYER failed, no spans CONFIRMED.
secret md -> BLOCKED, no spans, redaction_state=redacted, raw key absent from spans CONFIRMED.
repair queue: exactly the 3 repairable failures queued by source_id; secret+blob excluded CONFIRMED.
finish event ingestion_run_finished with rolled-up counts CONFIRMED.
MEDIUM cross-span secret leak: NOT exercised by any fixture (gap).
evidence_checked: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fixtures/knowledge_ingestion/mixed_root/** (8 committed files inspected; leaked_key.md is FAKE; no binary PDFs; no absolute paths)

hardening_pending.reason: Add a >120-line fixture with a MEDIUM secret straddling the window boundary; assert absent from all span content (would currently FAIL).

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
